name: Haldiram's
why_it_matters: Founded in Bikaner 1937 by Ganga Bishen Agarwal, Delhi's flagship at NSP Pitampura is the city's most-visited pure-veg restaurant chain. Sweets, namkeen, and a 100-dish thali under one roof — Delhi's default safe-bet for families who want everything.
insider_tip: The thali is the volume play (₹450 unlimited). The chaat counter is what locals queue at — the raj kachori is genuinely better here than at most street stalls.
signature_address: D Mall, Netaji Subhash Place, Pitampura, New Delhi 110034

name: Bikanervala
why_it_matters: Started in 1950 by Aggarwal brothers from Bikaner who set up the original Paranthe Wali Gali stall as Bikaner Bhujia Bhandar. Now headquartered at ITL Twin Tower NSP — 150 outlets globally but the Delhi flagship is where the family still oversees production.
insider_tip: Buy the namkeens fresh-fried at the counter (visible from the dining area) rather than packed; the difference in crunch is half the reason locals shop here. Sweets stay fresh 5-7 days at room temp.
signature_address: ITL Twin Tower, Netaji Subhash Place, Pitampura, New Delhi 110034

name: Nathu's Sweets
why_it_matters: Started in 1939 in Bengali Market — the Sundar Nagar branch occupies an entire restaurant floor above the sweet counter. Famous specifically for channa bhature; Delhi families have been ordering it since the 1950s.
insider_tip: First-floor restaurant is calmer than the ground-floor sweet counter chaos. Pao bhaji is the unsung order — closer to a Bombay rendition than most North Indian places.
signature_address: 2, Main Market, Sundar Nagar, New Delhi 110003

name: Evergreen Sweet House
why_it_matters: South Delhi's longest-running pure-veg sweet-and-chaat shop, opened 1963 next to the Green Park Metro. Halwais hand-rolling boondi laddoos visible through the front glass; same family for three generations.
insider_tip: Get there before 8pm or the chaat counter winds down. The boondi laddu is the take-away that locals send out-of-towners home with.
signature_address: S-29 & 30, Main Market, Green Park, near Green Park Metro Station, New Delhi 110016

name: Bhimsain's Bengali Sweet House
why_it_matters: Started 1936 by Lala Bhim Sain — the Bengali Market predecessor that gave the area its identity. Still family-run, sat next to its better-known namesake (Bengali Sweet House) but with its own loyal regulars.
insider_tip: Two Bengali Sweet Houses on the same block — Bhimsain's (this one) is at 27-29, the other at 30-33. Locals split: chaat-lovers go here, sweet-buyers go next door.
signature_address: 27-29, Bengali Market, Tansen Marg, Mandi House, New Delhi 110001

name: Aap Ki Pasand Tea Gallery (Sancha)
why_it_matters: India's oldest gourmet tea boutique, opposite Golcha Cinema since 1981. Master Tea Taster Sanjay Kapur's free tea-tasting sessions at a wooden gallery counter — Darjeeling, Assam, Nilgiri, Kangra, Munnar.
insider_tip: The free tasting is limited to 4-5 teas; ask for the seasonal first-flush Darjeeling. Buy by 100g rather than 250g — premium teas don't keep past 6 weeks once opened.
signature_address: Sterling House, 15 Netaji Subhash Marg, opposite Golcha Cinema, Daryaganj, New Delhi 110002

name: Dilli Haat Food Plaza
why_it_matters: 25 state-government food stalls under one roof — the only place in Delhi where you can taste Nagaland's bamboo-shoot chicken, Manipur's chamthong, Goa's fish curry, and Hyderabad's biryani in a single sitting. Allotments rotate so the line-up changes.
insider_tip: Go on a weekend afternoon when all 25 stalls are open. Skip the central food court and head to the rim stalls — Nagaland and Manipur stalls are usually the freshest and least-discovered.
signature_address: Dilli Haat INA, Aurobindo Marg, opposite INA Metro Station, New Delhi 110023

name: Banshi Di Hatti
why_it_matters: Banshi migrated from Rawalpindi at Partition, ran a stall outside Mall Road for years before settling in Malka Ganj in 1954. Three generations later, the chhole are still slow-boiled overnight in home masalas.
insider_tip: Open 8am till the dough runs out (usually noon). The aloo bhatura is the variant DU North Campus students rate highest — paneer is for the visitors.
signature_address: 5877, Jawahar Nagar, Malka Ganj Chowk, New Delhi 110007
